Hydra module that load-balance between symmetric Dataworks Enterprise sources.
used spread load client requests amoungst multiple sources that potentially provide same data.
Hydra commonly used where underlying data source suffers some form bottle neck associated with obtaining data, where this aliviated having multiple handlers access data.
example, when number requests made Global Topic Handler becomes very high Global Topic Server which connects drop updates (with notification) prevent buffer over flows.
solution this multiple GTHandlers against more Global Topic servers each mounting seperate sources.
Hydra then used combine these multiple logically equivalent sources into one, spreading requests GlobalTopic data amoungst each base source.
Hydra been standard Dataworks Enterprise component since Dataworks Enterprise release 1.0.0.137.
When client Hydra's out-going source makes request, hydra applies request available in-coming heads (input sources).
does this choosing 'least-loaded' head calculated basis number currently active requests each head.
Hydra also supports single backup head input source) which will direct requests when, only when, other heads unavailable.
this situation will return requests back onto first normal head that becomes available again.
Hydra also configured place limit maximum number requests that will apply given head.
this maximum reached available heads then further requests will given RTStale status will then queued until such time head becomes available that process request.
Configuration performed using Hydra.USR file.
specific details please consult installed configuration file.
Hydra presents just outpur source which behaviour underlying input sources.
input sources should equally capable servicing client request.
Hydra does rebalance requests once they made.
Requests always applied 'least-loaded' head time that request made remain that until request cancelled.
present Hydra sends client request least-loaded head calculated basis number currently active requests each head.
forthcoming version will able alternative, source-specific algorithim interogating each source relative loading factor using well-known instrument.
